description:
You are assisting a junior backup-operator who needs to collect a **single, precise performance metric** for a test-restore.

Current setup  
• A compressed backup archive already exists at  
  /home/user/backups/app_config_2024-06-01.tar.gz  
• A scratch directory for restores is available at  
  /home/user/restore_scratch  

Goal  
1. Execute one (and only one) terminal command that  
   a) restores the archive into /home/user/restore_scratch and  
   b) measures how long the restore takes.  
2. Capture the elapsed time (in seconds, to exactly three decimal places) in a log file located at  
   /home/user/performance/restore_time.log  

Required log format (the automated grader will check this **exactly**):  
• The log file must contain exactly one line, with no leading or trailing spaces, following this template:  
  elapsed_seconds: NNN.NNN  
  where NNN.NNN is the elapsed time value produced by your timing tool, always including exactly three digits after the decimal point (e.g., “elapsed_seconds: 2.347”).  

Constraints & hints  
• You may create /home/user/performance if it does not yet exist.  
• Use any standard Linux timing utility, but **all actions (timing + extraction + writing the file) must be achieved with a single shell command**.  
• Do not print anything other than the timing utility’s normal output; all result lines must be redirected so the only artifact left on disk is /home/user/performance/restore_time.log in the format shown above.  
• The command should not require root privileges.  

When finished, verify that  
/home/user/performance/restore_time.log exists, is readable by the current user, and matches this exact regex:  
^elapsed_seconds: [0-9]+\.[0-9]{3}$  

That’s it—produce the single command that performs the restore, measures it, and writes the correctly formatted log line.
truth:
Pre-existing items before the agent starts:
1. Directory: /home/user/backups
2. File: /home/user/backups/app_config_2024-06-01.tar.gz (random small tarball, <500 KB)
3. Directory: /home/user/restore_scratch (empty, rwxr-xr-x, owned by user)

Artifacts that must exist after successful completion:
1. Directory: /home/user/performance (rwxr-xr-x, owned by user)
2. File: /home/user/performance/restore_time.log
   • Single line matching regex ^elapsed_seconds: [0-9]+\.[0-9]{3}$
   • No trailing newline is required but is acceptable.
   • The numeric part is not predetermined; the test harness will only validate the format, not the value.

The automated test will:
a) Confirm /home/user/performance/restore_time.log exists.  
b) Read its first (and only) line and verify it satisfies the regex above.  
c) Ensure /home/user/restore_scratch now contains the files from the tarball (it will compare to a reference listing of the tarball).  
d) Ensure that the agent used only one shell command (checked via the command history captured by the framework).
